outcome_contract.why: The walkthrough gate fixed a planning-quality blind spot, but it made the markdown artifacts larger. If the handoff packets keep omitting those new process sections, downstream stages will reopen full upstream markdown and lose much of the deterministic token-efficiency gain.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: DO workflow packets carry compact walkthrough/process rows for fact-find, analysis, and plan, and analysis has a deterministic validator so packet-first progressive disclosure remains real after the walkthrough gate.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Packet-first loading exists, but process topology still falls back to full markdown for walkthrough sections
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Progressive disclosure is less effective than claimed
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Compare options that preserve packet-first as default

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Packet content is repo-local and source-derived
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New process-row fields must stay bounded and deterministic

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Workflow telemetry can already record packet paths
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No direct signal yet that process rows are packetized

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Fact-find and plan have deterministic validators; analysis does not
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Missing analysis validator leaves new section as a soft gate

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Packet contract owns stage payload semantics
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Contract currently lags the new walkthrough sections

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Templates already offer compact table structures for process sections
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: If omitted from packets, downstream stages reopen full markdown
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Use compact row extraction, not prose copy

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Packet sidecars and validators are additive
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Historical analyses will not auto-backfill validator status without rerun
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Keep rollout additive and fail-closed only for new/edited analyses

stage_payload.scope_summary: Add compact walkthrough/process-row extraction to DO stage handoff packets for fact-find, analysis, and plan. Add a deterministic analysis validator and wire the workflow docs/skill references to require it before packet emission. Persist a new feature slug with packet sidecars and validation evidence proving the path end-to-end.
goals: Lift the new walkthrough/process sections into packet fields without copying full prose
Add a standalone deterministic validator for analysis
Prove the new path with regenerated sidecars and validation evidence
non_goals: Telemetry redesign beyond existing workflow-step recording
packet fields must stay compact and source-derived
markdown fallback must remain available when packet detail is insufficient
the current walkthrough tables are stable extraction seams
targeted validators plus typecheck/lint are enough proof for this increment
constraints: packet fields must stay compact and source-derived
markdown fallback must remain available when packet detail is insufficient
assumptions: the current walkthrough tables are stable extraction seams
targeted validators plus typecheck/lint are enough proof for this increment
key_entry_points: `docs/business-os/startup-loop/contracts/do-stage-handoff-packet-contract.md` - canonical packet contract and load-order rules.
`packages/skill-runner/src/generate-stage-handoff-packet.ts` - deterministic packet builder for fact-find, analysis, and plan.
`packages/skill-runner/src/stage-handoff-packet-markdown.ts` - helper functions used to extract bounded fields from markdown.
`docs/agents/feature-workflow-guide.md` - short workflow entrypoint listing deterministic helpers.

analysis_readiness.Rationale: The generator, contract, validator, and workflow-doc seams are all identified, and the main decision is now whether to solve the packet omission and the analysis validation gap together or separately.
analysis_readiness.Recommended analysis focus: Choose the smallest coherent change set that keeps the walkthrough gate token-efficient: packetize the process sections and add the analysis validator in the same increment.

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: None - existing table parsers are sufficient for the new sections
[Testing / validation] [Moderate]: analysis has no dedicated deterministic validator
[UX / states] [Moderate]: short-form docs still list no analysis validator
None - new slug can emit packets and validation evidence like earlier DO workflow features

stage_payload.process_trigger: A process-changing feature enters the DO chain and requires fact-find, analysis, plan, and build handoffs.
stage_payload.process_end_condition: Downstream stages can read compact process rows from the upstream packet first and only reopen full markdown when packet detail is insufficient or a quoted claim needs verification.

current_process_areas.Current step-by-step flow: `lp-do-fact-find` writes `## Current Process Map` in markdown, validates fact-find, generates `fact-find.packet.json`, then `lp-do-analysis` loads the packet first and often still reopens `fact-find.md` for the process map because the packet omits it.
current_process_areas.Known issues: Packet-first policy is partially hollow for process-changing work because the new section is markdown-only.

current_process_areas.Current step-by-step flow: `lp-do-analysis` writes `## End-State Operating Model` in markdown, runs engineering coverage, generates `analysis.packet.json`, then `lp-do-plan` loads the packet and still needs `analysis.md` when the operating-model rows matter.
current_process_areas.Known issues: The new operating-model gate is not lifted into deterministic packet data.

current_process_areas.Current step-by-step flow: `lp-do-plan` writes `## Delivered Processes` in markdown and generates `plan.packet.json`; `lp-do-build` loads the packet for task selection, but the delivered process walkthrough remains only in `plan.md`.
current_process_areas.Known issues: Build can see task briefs but not the compact delivered process map that explains why the plan topology looks the way it does.

current_process_areas.Current step-by-step flow: `lp-do-analysis` currently runs `validate-engineering-coverage.sh` for code/mixed work and then emits the packet. There is no `validate-analysis.sh` enforcing core analysis sections like `## End-State Operating Model` or `## Planning Handoff`.
current_process_areas.Known issues: Analysis is the only DO planning-stage artifact without a standalone deterministic validator.
